steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: The blank sits between "including" and the noun phrase "the Madurese and Bikol." The whole list is already enclosed in em-dashes ("—including ______ the Madurese and Bikol, which...—"), so the parenthetical is already set off.

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
The whole phrase "—including ______ the Madurese and Bikol...—" sits inside em-dashes, which already mark it as a parenthetical. "Including" is a preposition; its object follows directly with no extra punctuation.

A — "languages": No punctuation after "languages" — the preposition "including" flows directly into its object ("the Madurese and Bikol"). Correct.

B — "languages--": A second em-dash inside a parenthetical already bounded by em-dashes creates a conflicting, unmatched pair and confuses the boundary of the parenthetical. Fails.

C — "languages:": A colon after a preposition ("including") is never correct — colons follow independent clauses, not prepositional phrases. Fails.

D — "languages,": A comma between the preposition "including" and its object interrupts the prepositional phrase unnecessarily. Fails.
